+function Invoke-CodexPreflightAudit {
+    # Opt-in pitfall audit: run Codex in a read-only sandbox, extract a
+    # marker-delimited Markdown body from the current audit output, validate
+    # the required headings and stable Pn / Vn IDs, write
+    # .ai/dispatch-<id>/codex.preflight.md only after validation succeeds,
+    # and return the validated checklist text for prompt injection.
+    #
+    # On any failure, Fail is called -- no codex.preflight.md is written,
+    # so a stale pre-existing checklist cannot be reused by a later step.
+    param([System.IO.FileInfo]$TaskPacket)
+
+    $taskRel = Get-RepoRelativePath $TaskPacket.FullName
+    $log = Join-Path $script:RunDir 'codex.preflight.log'
+    $checklistPath = Join-Path $script:RunDir 'codex.preflight.md'
+    $beginMarker = '<<<PREFLIGHT_AUDIT_BEGIN>>>'
+    $endMarker = '<<<PREFLIGHT_AUDIT_END>>>'
+
+    $prompt = @"
+You are Codex Pre-flight Auditor for an automated RGE dispatch loop.
+
+Read this TASK packet:
+
+$taskRel
+
+Your job is to identify scope-preserving pitfalls and verification focus items
+that will help Claude execute the TASK and Codex control review the result.
+You may inspect repository context needed to identify pitfalls. You must not
+edit files, stage, commit, or push. You must not expand scope: the TASK
+packet remains authoritative; this checklist is advisory only.
+
+Emit exactly one Markdown block between the two markers below. Do not write
+any other prose, headings, fences, or commentary outside the markers. Each
+marker must appear on its own line, anchored at column 1, exactly as shown.
+
+$beginMarker
+# Pre-flight Audit
+
+TASK_PACKET: $taskRel
+
+## Boundary
+
+The TASK packet remains authoritative. This checklist is advisory and must not
+expand scope.
+
+## Pitfall Checklist
+
+- [ ] P1: <scope-preserving pitfall text>
+- [ ] P2: <scope-preserving pitfall text>
+
+## Verification Checklist
+
+- [ ] V1: <verification focus text>
+- [ ] V2: <verification focus text>
+$endMarker
+
+Rules:
+- Use stable IDs P1, P2, P3, ... for the Pitfall Checklist items.
+- Use stable IDs V1, V2, V3, ... for the Verification Checklist items.
+- IDs must be unique within the audit (no duplicate Pn or Vn values).
+- Provide at least one Pn item and at least one Vn item.
+- Keep the four headings exactly as shown: ``# Pre-flight Audit``,
+  ``## Boundary``, ``## Pitfall Checklist``, ``## Verification Checklist``.
+- Do not authorize edits, tests, or scope changes beyond the TASK packet.
+"@
+
+    Invoke-CodexPrompt -Prompt $prompt -Sandbox 'read-only' -LogPath $log
+
+    if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $log)) {
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit produced no log file. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+    $logText = Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $log
+    if (-not $logText) {
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit log is empty. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+
+    # LastIndexOf so any earlier marker mention in Codex reasoning cannot
+    # outrank the final emitted block.
+    $startIdx = $logText.LastIndexOf($beginMarker)
+    if ($startIdx -lt 0) {
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit output is missing the begin marker '$beginMarker'. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+    $bodyStart = $startIdx + $beginMarker.Length
+    $endIdx = $logText.IndexOf($endMarker, $bodyStart)
+    if ($endIdx -lt 0) {
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit output is missing the end marker '$endMarker' after the begin marker. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+    $body = $logText.Substring($bodyStart, $endIdx - $bodyStart).Trim()
+    if (-not $body) {
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit body between markers is empty. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+
+    foreach ($required in @(
+        '# Pre-flight Audit',
+        '## Boundary',
+        '## Pitfall Checklist',
+        '## Verification Checklist'
+    )) {
+        $pattern = '(?m)^' + [regex]::Escape($required) + '\s*$'
+        if ($body -notmatch $pattern) {
+            Fail "Pre-flight audit body is missing required heading '$required'. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+        }
+    }
+
+    # Slice the body by '## ' section starts so item parsing cannot leak
+    # between sections.
+    $sections = [regex]::Split($body, '(?m)^(?=## )')
+    $pitfallText = ($sections | Where-Object { $_ -match '^## Pitfall Checklist\b' } | Select-Object -First 1)
+    $verifyText = ($sections | Where-Object { $_ -match '^## Verification Checklist\b' } | Select-Object -First 1)
+    if (-not $pitfallText -or -not $verifyText) {
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit body is missing a required checklist section. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+
+    $pItems = @()
+    foreach ($line in ($pitfallText -split "`r?`n")) {
+        if ($line -match '^\s*-\s*\[\s*\]\s*(P\d+)\s*:') {
+            $pItems += $matches[1]
+        }
+    }
+    $vItems = @()
+    foreach ($line in ($verifyText -split "`r?`n")) {
+        if ($line -match '^\s*-\s*\[\s*\]\s*(V\d+)\s*:') {
+            $vItems += $matches[1]
+        }
+    }
+
+    if ($pItems.Count -lt 1) {
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit has no '- [ ] Pn:' items in the Pitfall Checklist. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+    if ($vItems.Count -lt 1) {
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit has no '- [ ] Vn:' items in the Verification Checklist. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+
+    $pDupes = @($pItems | Group-Object | Where-Object { $_.Count -gt 1 })
+    if ($pDupes.Count -gt 0) {
+        $names = ($pDupes | ForEach-Object { $_.Name }) -join ', '
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit has duplicate P-number IDs: $names. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+    $vDupes = @($vItems | Group-Object | Where-Object { $_.Count -gt 1 })
+    if ($vDupes.Count -gt 0) {
+        $names = ($vDupes | ForEach-Object { $_.Name }) -join ', '
+        Fail "Pre-flight audit has duplicate V-number IDs: $names. See $(Get-RepoRelativePath $log)"
+    }
+
+    Write-TextFile $checklistPath $body
+    return $body
+}
+

+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-184
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-25T21:44:21+03:00
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 — added `-EnablePreflightAudit` switch, `Invoke-CodexPreflightAudit` helper, and conditional checklist injection into the Claude execute (round 0) and Codex control (every round) prompts
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_TASK_2026-05-25_21-31-00+0300.md — TASK packet executed
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_TASK_2026-05-25_21-31-00+0300.meta.json — TASK packet sidecar
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_EXEC_2026-05-25_21-44-08+0300.md — this report
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_TASK_2026-05-25_21-31-00+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`:
+  - Param block: added top-level `[switch]$EnablePreflightAudit` (default off,
+    no other parameter sets touched).
+  - Added function `Invoke-CodexPreflightAudit`: runs `Invoke-CodexPrompt`
+    with `-Sandbox read-only`, captures stdout to
+    `.ai/dispatch-<id>/codex.preflight.log`, extracts a marker-delimited
+    Markdown body (`<<<PREFLIGHT_AUDIT_BEGIN>>>` / `<<<PREFLIGHT_AUDIT_END>>>`,
+    last-occurrence anchored), validates the four required headings
+    (`# Pre-flight Audit`, `## Boundary`, `## Pitfall Checklist`,
+    `## Verification Checklist`), parses `- [ ] Pn:` and `- [ ] Vn:` items
+    from the right sections only, requires at least one of each, and fails
+    on duplicate Pn or Vn IDs. Writes
+    `.ai/dispatch-<id>/codex.preflight.md` only after every validation step
+    succeeds; on any failure `Fail` is invoked and no file is written, so a
+    stale pre-existing checklist cannot leak into a later step.
+  - Updated `Invoke-ClaudeExecute`: added `[string]$PreflightChecklist = ''`
+    parameter; when non-empty AND `Round -eq 0`, prepends an "Advisory
+    Codex pre-flight checklist (round 0 only)" block to the execute prompt,
+    followed by an explicit boundary statement that the TASK packet remains
+    authoritative, the checklist does not expand scope or authorize
+    out-of-envelope edits/tests, and conflicting items must be ignored. The
+    existing `EXEC_STATUS:` / `EXEC_PACKET:` tail marker contract and
+    Markers hashtable are unchanged.
+  - Updated `Invoke-CodexControl`: added `[string]$PreflightChecklist = ''`
+    parameter; when non-empty, injects the same advisory block plus the
+    boundary statement on every control round, placed between the
+    "Latest execution report:" header and the "Also inspect:" list. The
+    Codex JSON output schema (`.ai/codex_control.schema.json`) and
+    `--output-schema` / `--output-last-message` flow are unchanged.
+  - Main script body: after TASK finalization / resume location and after
+    the `-PlanOnly` exit, when `$EnablePreflightAudit` is set, calls
+    `Invoke-CodexPreflightAudit -TaskPacket $taskPacket` exactly once
+    before the execute loop. Stores the validated checklist in a local
+    `$preflightChecklist` variable that defaults to `''` so omitted-switch
+    invocations stay byte-identical to prior behavior, and threads that
+    variable into every `Invoke-ClaudeExecute` and `Invoke-CodexControl`
+    call inside the loop.
+
+`Invoke-CodexPrompt`, `Invoke-WithTimeout`, the stall watchdog,
+`CodexStallThresholdSec`, the canonical verification gate, queue/auto/
+scheduler/Rust/Cargo/docs/schemas, packet templates, and `.gitignore` were
+not touched. No new files were added other than this dispatch's own EXEC
+packet.
+
+### Tests
+
+None added. The TASK packet's Constraints / Non-Goals section explicitly
+forbids adding tests, dependencies, or fixtures. Behavior is verified via
+the PowerShell parser, `git diff --check`, and
+`.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1`.
+
+### Docs
+
+None added. The TASK packet forbids documentation changes for this
+dispatch.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Only one source file was edited; the bullets in `### Source` above are the
+complete description.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- PowerShell parser validation on `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+  (`[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile`) →
+  `$errors.Count` was 0; printed `PARSER OK`. Exit 0.
+- `git diff --check` → exit 0 (no whitespace errors, no conflict markers).
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0, final line `VERIFY OK: all 7
+  verification step(s) passed.` All 7 steps green:
+  1. `cargo +nightly fmt --check`
+  2. architecture lints (`rge-tool-architecture-lints all`)
+  3. `cargo test -p rge-tool-architecture-lints --all-targets`
+  4. `cargo build --workspace --all-targets`
+  5. `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast`
+  6. `cargo test --workspace --doc`
+  7. `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`
+
+The pre-flight audit feature itself was not exercised end-to-end inside
+this dispatch (the TASK packet's Planner Notes specifically state: "Do
+not recursively test this by launching another full dispatch unless a
+human explicitly asks for that. The required verification for this
+dispatch is the PowerShell parser check, `git diff --check`, and the
+canonical `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` gate.").
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. Only
+`Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` was modified; only this dispatch's own EXEC
+packet was added. No edits to queue, auto, scheduler, watchdog,
+verification, schema, or any other file outside the allowed surface.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- Marker choice: I picked `<<<PREFLIGHT_AUDIT_BEGIN>>>` /
+  `<<<PREFLIGHT_AUDIT_END>>>` and extract using `LastIndexOf` so that any
+  echo of the prompt in Codex reasoning cannot outrank the final emitted
+  block. If a different sentinel style is preferred, the change is
+  localized to `Invoke-CodexPreflightAudit`.
+- Prompt-injection ergonomics: the advisory block lands between the
+  packet reference and the "Protocol rules:" / "Also inspect:" sections.
+  Both prompts now include the explicit "TASK packet remains authoritative
+  / checklist is advisory / ignore on conflict" boundary statement.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` modified (uncommitted, per
+  protocol — orchestrator dispatches never commit).

@@ -0,0 +1,296 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-184
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-25_21-31-00+0300
+REVISION: 0
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_TASK_2026-05-25_21-31-00+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_TASK_2026-05-25_21-31-00+0300.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_CORRECT_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_CORRECT_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+- GitHub issue #184
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Add an opt-in Codex pre-flight pitfall audit to `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` for
+GitHub issue #184. When explicitly enabled, the loop should ask Codex to review
+the finalized TASK packet in a read-only sandbox before Claude execution, save a
+validated checklist as `codex.preflight.md`, and surface that checklist to
+Claude and Codex control as a non-authoritative aid. Existing dispatch behavior
+must be unchanged unless the new switch is supplied.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- This dispatch's own generated handoff artifacts only:
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_TASK_2026-05-25_21-31-00+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_TASK_2026-05-25_21-31-00+0300.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_EXEC_*.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_EXEC_*.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_CORRECT_*.md`, and
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_CORRECT_*.meta.json`.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one. Do not manually create or edit unrelated dispatch logs.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `new-handoff.ps1`
+- `.ai/**`, including `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`,
+  `.ai/codex_control.schema.json`, `.ai/handoff.schema.json`, and any other
+  schema file
+- `ai_handoffs/**`, except this dispatch's own `ISSUE-184_TASK`,
+  `ISSUE-184_EXEC`, and `ISSUE-184_CORRECT` Markdown packets and matching
+  `.meta.json` sidecars listed above
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/**`, except this dispatch's own exact queue log artifact if
+  the queue runner creates one
+- The `Invoke-CodexPrompt` function signature or body
+- The stall watchdog implementation, `Invoke-WithTimeout` behavior,
+  `CodexStallThresholdSec`, timeout handling, or any timeout result shape
+- `codex_control.schema.json`, output schemas, handoff schemas, packet
+  templates, queue publish gates, auto-selection behavior, scheduler behavior,
+  Rust source, Cargo manifests, `Cargo.lock`, tests, fixtures, workflows, docs,
+  plans, status files, architecture-lint files, `.gitignore`, and repository
+  metadata
+
+### MAY add new files
+
+- This dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_EXEC_*.md` execution
+  report and matching generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+  sidecar.
+- This dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_CORRECT_*.md`
+  correction packet and matching generated
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_CORRECT_*.meta.json` sidecar, only if a correction
+  round is required by the orchestrator.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+
+- Any new source file, test file, fixture, generated asset, manifest, lockfile,
+  dependency file, crate, workflow, schema, script, doc, ADR, plan, status file,
+  architecture-lint artifact, `.gitignore` entry, or repository metadata file.
+- Any handoff packet or sidecar for another dispatch.
+- Any `REVIEW` or `CLOSEOUT` packet for this orchestrator dispatch.
+- Any `ai_dispatch_logs/**` file other than this dispatch's own exact queue log
+  artifact if the queue runner creates one.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Add a top-level switch parameter named `EnablePreflightAudit` to
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`. Its default must be off, and existing
+  invocations that do not pass the switch must not run the audit, write
+  `codex.preflight.md`, or add any pre-flight audit text to Claude or Codex
+  prompts.
+- When `EnablePreflightAudit` is supplied for an execution-capable run, run one
+  Codex pre-flight audit after the approved TASK packet is available and before
+  Claude execute round 0. This applies to both normal plan-then-execute runs
+  and `-ResumeApprovedTask` runs. `-PlanOnly` must retain its current
+  stop-after-finalized-TASK behavior and must not run the audit.
+- Run the pre-flight Codex audit with a read-only Codex sandbox. The audit may
+  inspect the finalized TASK packet and repository context needed to identify
+  scope-preserving pitfalls, but it must not edit files, stage, commit, push, or
+  require any file outside the allowed implementation surface.
+- Write the successful extracted audit artifact to exactly
+  `.ai/dispatch-$DispatchId/codex.preflight.md`. Do not create or overwrite
+  that file until extraction and validation of the current audit output
+  succeeds. Do not use a stale pre-existing `codex.preflight.md` as the
+  checklist for a new run.
+- Use this required Pre-flight Audit Markdown shape in `codex.preflight.md`:
+
+```markdown
+# Pre-flight Audit
+
+TASK_PACKET: ai_handoffs/ISSUE-184_TASK_2026-05-25_21-31-00+0300.md
+
+## Boundary
+
+The TASK packet remains authoritative. This checklist is advisory and must not
+expand scope.
+
+## Pitfall Checklist
+
+- [ ] P1: Scope-preserving pitfall text.
+- [ ] P2: Scope-preserving pitfall text.
+
+## Verification Checklist
+
+- [ ] V1: Verification focus text.
+- [ ] V2: Verification focus text.
+```
+
+- The extracted checklist IDs must be stable P-number and V-number IDs:
+  `P1`, `P2`, and so on for pitfalls, and `V1`, `V2`, and so on for
+  verification focus items. IDs must be unique within the audit, must not be
+  regenerated during prompt injection, and must remain visible wherever the
+  checklist is injected.
+- Validate extraction before writing `codex.preflight.md`. At minimum,
+  extraction succeeds only when the current Codex output contains the required
+  Markdown shape, has a `## Pitfall Checklist` section with one or more
+  `- [ ] Pn:` items, has a `## Verification Checklist` section with one or
+  more `- [ ] Vn:` items, and has no duplicate P-number or V-number IDs.
+- If the pre-flight Codex command fails or extraction/validation fails, fail the
+  dispatch before Claude execution with a clear message naming the pre-flight
+  audit log or extraction failure. Do not write a partial
+  `codex.preflight.md`, do not inject a stale checklist, and do not continue as
+  though the requested opt-in audit succeeded.
+- Inject the extracted checklist into the Claude execute prompt only for round
+  0, only when a current checklist exists, and without changing the
+  `EXEC_STATUS` / `EXEC_PACKET` marker contract. Do not inject it into Claude
+  correction-round execute prompts.
+- Inject the extracted checklist into the Codex control prompt on every control
+  round when a current checklist exists, without changing
+  `.ai/codex_control.schema.json` or the required JSON response shape.
+- In both the Claude execute prompt and the Codex control prompt, state
+  explicitly that the TASK packet remains authoritative and that the pre-flight
+  checklist is advisory, must not expand scope, and must be ignored if it
+  conflicts with the TASK packet.
+- Do not modify `Invoke-CodexPrompt`; reuse it as-is or add separate helper
+  logic around it. Do not modify the stall watchdog, timeout behavior, or any
+  schema.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only source file changed is `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`.
+- With `EnablePreflightAudit` omitted, behavior is unchanged: no pre-flight
+  Codex audit command runs, no `codex.preflight.md` is written, no checklist is
+  injected into Claude execute prompts, and no checklist is injected into Codex
+  control prompts.
+- With `EnablePreflightAudit` supplied for a normal execution run, the audit
+  runs exactly once after TASK approval/finalization and before Claude execute
+  round 0.
+- With `EnablePreflightAudit` supplied for `-ResumeApprovedTask`, the audit
+  runs exactly once after the approved TASK is located and before Claude execute
+  round 0.
+- With `-PlanOnly`, the run still stops after the approved TASK and does not
+  run the pre-flight audit or write `codex.preflight.md`.
+- The pre-flight Codex audit is invoked with `--sandbox read-only`.
+- `codex.preflight.md` is written only after current-output extraction and
+  validation succeed. Failed extraction does not leave a partial checklist file
+  and cannot cause stale checklist injection.
+- The audit Markdown uses the required `# Pre-flight Audit`,
+  `## Boundary`, `## Pitfall Checklist`, and `## Verification Checklist`
+  headings, and all checklist items keep unique stable `Pn` or `Vn` IDs.
+- Claude execute round 0 receives the checklist only when it exists and sees a
+  prompt statement that the TASK packet remains authoritative and the checklist
+  must not expand scope.
+- Claude execute rounds greater than 0 do not receive the pre-flight checklist.
+- Codex control receives the checklist on every round when it exists and sees a
+  prompt statement that the TASK packet remains authoritative and the checklist
+  must not expand scope.
+- The Codex control response schema and `.ai/codex_control.schema.json` remain
+  unchanged.
+- `Invoke-CodexPrompt`, `Invoke-WithTimeout`, the stall watchdog,
+  `CodexStallThresholdSec`, timeout exit codes, Claude marker parsing,
+  verification behavior, queue behavior, auto-driver behavior, scheduler
+  behavior, Rust/Cargo files, docs, and schemas remain unchanged.
+- The implementation remains compatible with Windows PowerShell 5.1 and does
+  not use PowerShell 7-only syntax or APIs.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This dispatch is only for an opt-in pre-flight Codex pitfall audit inside
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`.
+- The pre-flight checklist is not a second task packet, not a correction
+  packet, not a schema, and not a new source of scope authority.
+- Do not add default-on audit behavior, queue pass-through parameters, auto
+  driver pass-through parameters, scheduled-task parameters, documentation, or
+  schema changes.
+- Do not change `Invoke-CodexPrompt`, the stall watchdog, native-command
+  timeout behavior, or any existing Codex planning/control invocation contract
+  except for conditional prompt text around the new checklist.
+- Do not add tests, dependencies, external tools, generated fixtures, new
+  packet templates, or new documentation.
+- Do not use this dispatch to alter task packet finalization, handoff sidecar
+  generation, control-review JSON semantics, queue staging/publishing policy,
+  or the canonical verification gate.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
+`EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, expected exit
+  0:
+
+```powershell
+$tokens = $null
+$errors = $null
+[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile(
+  (Resolve-Path '.\Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1'),
+  [ref]$tokens,
+  [ref]$errors
+) | Out-Null
+if ($errors.Count -ne 0) { $errors | Format-List *; exit 1 }
+```
+
+- `git diff --check` is expected to exit 0.
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` is expected to exit 0 and report `VERIFY OK`.
+- Inspect the final diff and document that changed files are limited to
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` plus this dispatch's own allowed handoff and
+  exact queue-log artifacts.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of
+the following occur:
+
+- The requested feature cannot be implemented by editing only
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` plus this dispatch's own generated artifacts.
+- Adding `EnablePreflightAudit` would change behavior for invocations that do
+  not pass the switch.
+- The audit cannot be run in a read-only Codex sandbox.
+- The implementation cannot write `codex.preflight.md` only after successful
+  current-output extraction and validation.
+- The checklist cannot preserve stable `Pn` and `Vn` IDs through both artifact
+  writing and prompt injection.
+- The Claude execute prompt cannot limit checklist injection to round 0 only.
+- The Codex control prompt cannot receive the checklist on every round without
+  changing `.ai/codex_control.schema.json` or the JSON response shape.
+- The implementation would require changing `Invoke-CodexPrompt`, the stall
+  watchdog, timeout behavior, queue, auto, scheduler, Rust, Cargo, docs,
+  schemas, packet templates, verification scripts, or any file outside the
+  allowed edit set.
+- PowerShell parser validation, `git diff --check`, or
+  `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` fails for a reason that cannot be fixed inside
+  the allowed edit set.
+- Any edit outside the allowed file/artifact set appears. Do not clean or
+  revert unrelated user work; halt and report the deviation.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+Prefer a small helper in `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` that runs the read-only
+Codex audit, extracts a marker-delimited Markdown body from the current audit
+output, validates the required headings and IDs, writes
+`codex.preflight.md` only after validation, and returns the checklist text for
+conditional prompt injection.
+
+The prompt text injected into Claude and Codex should be concise. Preserve the
+P-number and V-number IDs so control can refer to them clearly, but make the
+boundary statement hard to miss: the TASK packet remains authoritative, and the
+checklist cannot authorize edits or tests outside the TASK packet.
+
+Do not recursively test this by launching another full dispatch unless a human
+explicitly asks for that. The required verification for this dispatch is the
+PowerShell parser check, `git diff --check`, and the canonical
+`.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` gate.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+NEXT_ROLE: EXECUTOR_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---
